title: Independently assess this dangerous operation request and return only a compact JSON object with keys vote, risk_assessment, reasoning, conditions, alternatives. Operation: `git push --force origin feature`. Criteria: purpose clarity, blast radius, reversibility, alternatives, security. Assume no fu...
summary_text: {"vote":"no-go (until conditions met)","risk_assessment":"high","reasoning":"Assumptions: no additional context, unknown branch ownership, unknown protection rules, unknown CI/release coupling. `git push --force origin feature` rewrites remote history, can delete others’ commits, disrupt PR/CI references, and weakens audit integrity (security/compliance concern) if used without controls. Reversibility is uncertain unless exact prior SHAs/backups are captured.","conditions":["State explicit purpose (e.g., remove secret or fix bad rebase) and why non-destructive options fail.","Confirm `feature` is exclusively owned or all contributors explicitly approved.","Create recoverability point first (tag/backup ref of current remote head).","Use `git push --force-with-lease=refs/heads/feature:<expected_sha>` instead of plain `--force`.","Verify branch protections/policy allow history rewrite and announce maintenance window."],"alternatives":["`git revert` to undo bad commits without rewriting history.","Push corrected history to a new branch and open a replacement PR.","Interactive rebase locally, then `--force-with-lease` only if rewrite is strictly required.","Ask admin to temporarily lock/coordinate branch updates, then perform controlled rewrite."]}
